skill.name: converting-email-templates
skill.description: Convert email templates from SendGrid, Mailgun, Mandrill, Postmark, Brevo, and Amazon SES into Mailtrap-compatible Handlebars syntax.
skill.summary: Convert provider-specific email templates into Mailtrap Handlebars templates with reports for manual review.
skill.author: mailtrap

security_audit.summary: Static analysis produced many high-risk matches, but most are false positives from Markdown examples, Handlebars syntax, and provider terminology. The real risks are legitimate for this skill: it asks the agent to run a bundled Python converter, read and write local template files, and optionally use Mailtrap API examples with bearer tokens.

medium_findings.title: Bundled Converter Script Executes Locally
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to run scripts/converter.py through python3. The script reads user-selected HTML files, creates output directories, and writes converted files plus reports. This is legitimate for template migration, but users should understand it performs local filesystem operations.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct instructions execute a bundled Python script, and the script contains clear file read and write operations. The behavior matches the skill purpose, so the risk is operational rather than malicious.

medium_findings.title: Mailtrap API Examples Use Bearer Tokens
medium_findings.description: The migration guide includes curl examples for creating, listing, updating, deleting, and sending templates through Mailtrap API endpoints. The examples use MAILTRAP_API_TOKEN and MAILTRAP_ACCOUNT_ID environment variables, which is safer than hardcoding but still involves live network calls if executed.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network endpoints and bearer token headers are explicit in the documentation. They target Mailtrap service APIs and are consistent with the skill purpose, not hidden exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Static Code Execution Matches Are Mostly Markup False Positives
low_findings.description: The repeated Ruby backtick and obfuscation detections are caused by Markdown code spans, Handlebars braces, regex examples, and template conversion tables. No Ruby backtick execution or JavaScript obfuscation behavior was found in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is visible as documentation, Handlebars syntax, or Python regex strings. I found no semantic evidence that these matches execute shell commands.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable References Are Secret Handling Guidance
low_findings.description: The guide references MAILTRAP_API_TOKEN, MAILTRAP_ACCOUNT_ID, .env, and secret managers. This is guidance for avoiding hardcoded credentials, not code that reads or exfiltrates environment files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The references appear in user-facing setup instructions and curl examples. No code path reads .env files or enumerates environment variables.

low_findings.title: No Prompt Injection Attempt Found
low_findings.description: Reviewed skill instructions, migration content, conversion rules, and converter script for override language or fake system messages. No evidence found for instructions that attempt to alter the security review process.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed top-level instructions describe email template conversion and do not contain suspicious override phrases. This is a negative finding, so confidence is high but not absolute.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Script Invocation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill workflow calls python3 with a bundled converter script and user-supplied provider, input, and output paths.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command is shown directly in the workflow. It is intentional and bounded to conversion, but it is still executable behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local File Read and Write Operations
dangerous_patterns.description: The converter reads input HTML and writes converted HTML, report files, and a bulk summary into the selected output directory.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file operations are explicit Path read_text and write_text calls. They are expected for this utility but should be disclosed.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documented Network Requests With Authorization Header
dangerous_patterns.description: The guide includes curl commands that call Mailtrap API endpoints with an Authorization bearer token from the environment.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The network calls are documentation examples and use first-party Mailtrap endpoints. They become real network activity only if the user runs them.

content.value_statement: Migrating templates between email providers often breaks variables, conditionals, and API field mappings. This skill converts common provider syntax to Mailtrap Handlebars and reports items that need manual review.
actual_capabilities: Converts SendGrid insert helpers into Mailtrap-compatible Handlebars fallbacks.
Converts Mailchimp merge tags used by Mandrill into lower-case Handlebars variables.
Converts Postmark Mustachio conditionals and unescaped variables where matching is clear.
Converts Brevo params variables, conditionals, loops, and default filters into Handlebars patterns.
Processes a single HTML file or all .html files in a directory through the bundled converter script.
Generates conversion reports that list changed lines and patterns requiring manual review.
limitations: It does not import templates into Mailtrap automatically unless the user runs the documented API steps.
It cannot safely convert provider-specific date formatting, comparison helpers, or complex filters without application changes.
It only processes .html files during bulk directory conversion.
Postmark closing tags and nested Brevo loops may require manual verification after conversion.

use_cases.title: Move Marketing Templates From SendGrid
use_cases.description: Convert exported SendGrid HTML templates and identify helpers that need server-side replacement before import.

use_cases.title: Standardize Transactional Templates
use_cases.description: Migrate order, password reset, and welcome templates from several providers into one Mailtrap Handlebars format.

use_cases.title: Review Complex Template Syntax
use_cases.description: Use the generated reports to find unsupported filters, loops, and conditionals before a production migration.

prompt_templates.title: Convert One Template
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this SendGrid template file to Mailtrap Handlebars. Use provider sendgrid, input path ./welcome.html, and output path ./converted.

prompt_templates.title: Convert a Template Folder
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert all Postmark .html templates in ./postmark-exports to Mailtrap format. Put the results and reports in ./mailtrap-converted.

prompt_templates.title: Review Flagged Conversion Items
prompt_templates.prompt: Read the conversion reports in ./converted and group the flags by application code changes, template fixes, and items that need verification.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Provider Migration
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit these Brevo templates for Mailtrap migration. Convert what can be converted, explain unsupported filters, and map the API fields I must update.

output_examples.input: A SendGrid welcome template with insert defaults and date formatting helpers.
output_examples.output: A Mailtrap-ready Handlebars template, plus a report that lists converted fallback variables and date helpers that require server-side formatting.

output_examples.input: A directory of Postmark HTML templates exported from a production project.
output: Converted template files in the selected output directory.
One report per template with change counts and manual review flags.
A bulk summary showing clean files and files that need verification.

output_examples.input: A Brevo order confirmation template with params variables, loops, and filters.
output_examples.output: A converted Handlebars template with params prefixes removed, loops rewritten, and unsupported filters flagged for application-level changes.

best_practices: Run the converter on copied exports and keep the original provider templates unchanged.
Review every generated report before importing templates into Mailtrap.
Move date formatting, comparisons, and complex logic into application code before final testing.
anti_patterns: Do not paste real API tokens directly into templates, reports, or prompts.
Do not treat flagged syntax as production-ready without review.
Do not mix templates from different source providers in one conversion batch.

faq.question: Which providers does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports SendGrid, Mailgun, Mandrill, Postmark, Brevo, and Amazon SES template migration to Mailtrap Handlebars.

faq.question: Does it send emails for me?
faq.answer: No. It converts templates and documents Mailtrap API changes. Email sending only happens if you run the provided API examples.

faq.question: Can it process many templates at once?
faq.answer: Yes. The converter processes all .html files in a selected directory and writes a bulk summary.

faq.question: What happens to unsupported provider helpers?
faq.answer: Unsupported helpers are flagged in reports. Some are commented out in converted output to prevent Mailtrap editor errors.

faq.question: Does it change my original files?
faq.answer: No. The workflow writes converted files to the selected output directory and keeps original files unchanged.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
